Alright, so now we are going to dig into handwritten cards. Alright, so what we're going to be going over in this is going to be key factors. Okay, so those are going to be when to send, who should write the card, what they should say, and how to track. Okay, so handwritten cards are just an amazing way to over deliver. Your clients will not be expecting them and it builds a ton of goodwill and value between you and your members. So them just getting home, opening up a handwritten card that's super specific about something that happened at their gym is a great way to make them feel awesome and build that relationship and it really helps with retention. So let's go over how we're going to do this. First card that goes out, that's going to go out once someone signs up for your challenge. Okay, sent by the person who signed them up, so your sales manager or the owner or whoever they sat down with during orientation and talked to. That one's going to reference something specific about the conversation. So a couple ways to do that is to take notes during the conversation or if they're filling out a pre-question there, you can easily just keep that or make a copy of it or we typically keep them and then just reference something that they wrote down on there. Like what's your main goal? Oh, I want to get in shape for my sister's wedding. Right, that's perfect to add to the card because they're going to be like, man, that guy really remembered what I was talking about with him. So I'm super pumped to do this thing. So something specific about the conversation and that is going to be tracked on the challengers tab of your spreadsheet which we will be going into next. Card number two, once someone reaches the halfway point of your challenge and this is going to be sent out by your challenge coach or your challenge manager, whoever you have running your challenges. If it is still the owner, then that's the person that will send it out and this one is going to be either a congratulations on the progress that they've made or a pick me up if they are not doing so well. So if they're not, you know, visiting enough, if they're not getting the results, if if something isn't going perfect, this is a great chance to just, you know, cheer them on and try to get their head back in the game. Otherwise, if they're doing amazing, like this is a great way to, you know, feed that momentum and keep their progress going all the way to the end. So this, again, is going to be tracked on the challengers tab of the spreadsheet and we're covering all the spreadsheets next. Card number three, once someone finishes your challenge, this one should be sent by your retention manager. This is a great way for them to kind of introduce themselves and build value with them and your members. Again, if it's the owner or somebody else that already wrote a card, that's completely fine. The point of this card is congratulating them on finishing and then welcoming them to the family. So I like to write these cards, even if they are not going to be continuing for whatever reason, just because it builds good will and if they are looking for a gym in the future, this can only help them making a decision to come back to you guys. So, but this one should be going out to the person that is going to be mostly responsible for retaining them and sending them messages, reach-outs on an ongoing basis, whoever that would be. A lot of the cases, it'll be like a retention manager. Again, this one is tracked on the challengers tab of the spreadsheet. Card number four and on, like ongoing, these go out every three months. So the first card number four would go out three months after the retention manager or card number three went out. Okay, and we'll show you exactly how to track that really easily. This one is going to be sent by your trainers or your coaches and this is going to reference something specific about them. So reaching a goal, maybe, if they're working with a trainer and they hit, you know, 20 pounds lost, class progress. So referencing something that happened in class, like, hey Mrs. Jones, I just wanted to let you know that you're doing so great. Like I remember when you could only do one push-up and now you can do, you know, 20, that's just fantastic and that'll make them feel like really pumped or just something that happened in class that day. Like, hey, wasn't that funny, you know, blah blah blah. And then so it builds a lot of value when the card is specific. If it's just a generalized card and it's just saying, hey, keep up the great work, that's not going to be very effective and it's probably not worth all the work that's going into this system. So on the spreadsheet, which we'll cover next, I can, we'll show you how to, how the trainers will track exactly what they're writing in the card. So you as an owner can just pop in there and see exactly what the message was. So this one is going to be tracked on the bootcamp or trainer tab of the spreadsheet. So bootcamp, if they are just a normal large group, and it could say large group, large group member, or if they are a HTTP or an online trainer, I'm sorry, member, then this one will be tracked directly with the trainer that they're working with. The reason we have two is because for large group, a lot of the times you'll do like around Robin and whatever trainer has to do cards that week or if they're just picking any one of them, it's just a big pool of all of your large group members. Whereas on the trainer tab, it's going to be specific to the person that they're assigned to, which is exactly the case in HTTP or online. So that is all we are going to cover as far as this portion goes. And now we are going to jump into the spreadsheet itself. All right, welcome to the next video in retention. And we are going to be going over handwritten cards. So handwritten cards are amazing ways for you to reach out to your clients and just over deliver because they're not expecting it. And it's super cool for them to get a handwritten card in the mail from you or one of your trainers that is really like heartfelt and actually references something that happened at the gym or progress that they've made or results that they've gotten. So that's going to be the key behind handwritten cards is not just doing them, but actually having the content be something really good. So that's why we created this tracking sheet. So you as an owner, if you're not personally writing them, you can go in and you can see the message that was written to who it was written to. And so you'll, you'll share this sheet with your staff and they will type in the message or they can hand you the card after they've written it and you can type it in just to verify what the message is and then who it goes to. So let me kind of show you how it works. The first thing that we're going to do is explain the tabs on the bottom and then we'll talk about when people are getting handwritten cards. So you can see the tab that we're on now is called challengers. Okay. So with the challengers, we want to send them four cards. I'm sorry, three cards, a sign up card. So this sign up card is after they sign up for your challenge, you want to thank them and welcome them to the team or to the family and then wish them luck on their challenge. Okay. So this could be done or should be done by the person that sold them into the challenge, whether that's yourself or your sales manager or whoever, they should write a card because that's who it makes the most sense for. Then we should write them a card at the halfway point. So after they've done the abandoned hope meeting, hopefully they decided to continue with you. And I would definitely reference that after the meeting in the card or if they have not attended the meeting or anything like that, you should write them a card just telling them how amazing they're doing. And this should come from whoever's running the challenge. So if it's you, then you can write them a second card. But this time it's not about like joining the program, it's more so about continuing the momentum that they've built over the first few weeks. Okay. So that is who is going to be writing the second card. Whoops. And then the final card that you'll be reading or writing to a challenger is a welcome card once they become an EFT. So if they do not become an EFT, you can send them like a thanks for trying out our program just to keep good will. Or mostly you want to just write the people that are continuing onto your program and welcome them to the next however long they've signed up for or welcome them to working with the new trainers or coaches or however, whatever makes the most sense for your gym. That's what you want to reference in the card. So with that being said, the final, the final here is just totals. It's just totals of cards that went out and you want to be able to calculate everything. So you know how many cards as an example, if you signed up 60 challengers this month, you should have 60 cards that went out. So let's show you how to track it. So we go on the left hand side and first thing that we're going to do is you're going to put in all of your challenger's names. So these are just the names of the people that are signed up for your challenge. So we can put Josh and Mike and Suzy and Michelle. Okay. So now you can see in the top it automatically will calculate up here to four. So four cards need to be written. Okay. And we need four cards on the halfway and we need four final cards. Okay. Because none of them were written. Now, as soon as a card is written, you can put in a date that it was written. So let's say it's 520. All right. Now one card has been written and three are still needs to be written. Okay. And we'll put down who wrote them Joey. And then that person will put in the message. Okay. And that's going to be like, wow, they took time out of their day to like wrote me that or write me that. That's awesome. Whoever is going to mail it as soon as you check that. Now there is only three cards left to be mailed out, which would be Mike Suzy's and Michelle's. Okay. But there's still the four on the halfway cards and there's still four on there. So all you're doing is working your way down the list for the challenger experience, right. So which one of your trainers wrote cards and how many cards they wrote. Right? And so the key is that you want to be able to make sure all of your trainers are writing the amount of cards that you have set for them. They just, you know, because you want to receive them on a weekly basis. So what I do as a gym owner is I like to put in all of the client's names, which I just grab off of the challenge, I'm sorry, off the master sheets and I just plop them in here and then my trainers have to pick whoever they want to write a card to. That way I never, I never forget. So it goes all the way down to, I literally just paste all my client's names all the way down to, I don't know what it goes down to, you know, 600. However many clients you have, 500, 400, 300. Paste them all in there. That way nobody gets a second card until everybody's got their first card. Right. And then once everybody has been mailed out, all the buttons are clicked. You erase them all and you start again. So depending on how many clients you have, that's how many cards you guys need to do per week. So you want a client to get a card every three months. Once they, if you want to be more aggressive every two months, you know, every month I think is a little excessive to be getting a card every month from your gym, but every three months is kind of nice. And so just as an example, if you have 300 clients, right, then you need to mail out 100 cards per month. So if you have, that's going to be 25 cards per week, roughly. Right. To hit that cadence. And so if you do 25 cards per week and you have five trainers, each trainer needs to do five. Now I have my whole staff do it. So if I have managers, if I have whoever, that way our goal is going to be two per week that each person needs to do, which is super easy. And it's just a personal experience and they get to pick whoever they want to write to. So then they fill out, again, these sheets are shared with my staff so they can type in exactly what they wrote. If your staff, if you don't want to do it like that, I've also done it where whoever's mailing them out, they'll type the message in more of an administrative role type of deal. So before you mail it out, type it in here as almost as like a quality control check to make sure it looks nice. Because if they're just, if your trainers are just like, Hey, keep up the good work. There's no point in, you're not going to get that personal connection that you're going for. So you're just doing all this busy work. It's not going to really help drive down retention. Right. And the whole point is to drive down retention. So they need to be personalized. A handwritten card that's personalized with a message that pertains only to that person. It's almost to the point to where if you accidentally sent that card to someone else, it wouldn't make sense to be like, why did he write this? Like that doesn't, that never happened to me. Right. That's how personalized you want it to be. Right. And then this on the right hand side is to keep your staff accountable. If these are unchecked, then you can talk to your ops manager and say, Hey, why is there three cards left to be mailed? Do you need stamps or something? So that way it's all set to go. So that's exactly what you do for all of your, your large group, all of your, your classes. However, if you also do you know, HTTP or one on one, you don't want to forget those people as well. So it makes sense to have a tracking system for them, which brings us to our final tab, which is trainer clients. Okay. And so this tab, you would just duplicate so you can duplicate and for however many staff members you have, you know, and just rename it. So instead of saying trainer clients, you can just rename it Bob's clients, Justin's clients, Alex's clients, however many you have. And this is for them to, to keep track as well. It's the same thing as the challengers, but this is if you onboard somebody directly to HTTP or where they have that really great connection with them, you want that, that trainer to send them a card for signing up. This could also be the salesperson that signed them up. Again, a halfway card through their, you know, through their first 12 weeks, or however you're doing, if it's one on one, if it's online clients as well, whoever they're working with sends them a card at the halfway point, and then a welcome card to the team. So after you, after you do that, just make sure that you are continuing to send out the cards on a regular basis to the clients, the one on one clients or the HTTP clients, and you're always checking your, you're always checking in to make sure that your trainers have signed them all in. So with that being said, I do want to show you like a copy of a handwritten card and how you can get those. So this would just be, is the ones that we use. So it's just a regular card. We get them printed off. You can get them printed on like Amazon or Vista print or anything like that. And you just open it up. And all it says is thanks for your support. And then the bottom is just completely blank. So you can write whatever you want in there. Pretty simple card. Got a little thing on the back. Has our contact info. And then we have envelopes. If you do envelopes, make sure you have the return address. So you don't have to write it on there. You just write the name. And it's just a card fits right in the envelope. Super simple. I would get 500 of them printed off in a batch and you'll have them for a long time. That's, you can, there's a lot of sources to get handwritten cards, a lot of printers that'll do it. So that's all that we're going to go over for handwritten cards is the tracking system. Make sure you show this to your trainers. I would encourage you maybe filming it. So in case they forget, you can send them the video. But that's exactly how you do it. You should stay super organized. Remember to always leave a couple of them blank so you can duplicate the blank ones. And we'll keep it locked so you can't accidentally delete the formulas that move everything over and you should be good to go. All right, guys, we're going to go into the next training.
